---
# =====================================================================
#  Press release — one-page PDF (LaTeX/PDF via Quarto)
#  Conventions: see style-guides/press-release/README.md
#  Render:  quarto render press-release-template.qmd
#  Design system: ../research-highlight/_style.tex (shared colours, fonts, macros)
#
#  SHAPE — inverted pyramid, third person, built to be quoted/reprinted.
#  Most newsworthy first; an editor should get the story from the lead alone.
#
#  LENGTH (target):
#    Headline <=15w | Subhead <=20w | Lead ~50w
#    Body 2-4 paras (~250-350w) | Quotes 1-3 (~30-50w ea) | Boilerplate ~50-75w
# =====================================================================
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - top=0.6in
      - bottom=0.55in
      - left=0.75in
      - right=0.75in
    include-in-header:
      - file: ../research-highlight/_style.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{=latex}
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MASTHEAD  (logo left, label right, 2.2pt ink rule beneath)
% Update the logo path when copied into a paper's press-release/ folder:
%   ../../../formats/logos/eil-logo-maroon.png
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\briefheader{\includegraphics[height=0.45in]{../logos/eil-logo-maroon.png}}{Press Release}

% Body type for the release (overrides the _style.tex 9.4pt default)
\fontsize{11pt}{15pt}\selectfont

% Gap below the masthead rule to the headline (~7pt, matching the research
% highlight's headsep; the briefheader macro adds -3pt, so 10pt nets ~7pt)
\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% HEADLINE + SUBHEAD  (headline = the finding as news, not a topic)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\brieftitle{[Headline — the finding as news, <=15 words]}
\vspace{1pt}
\noindent{\color{cite}\rmfamily\itshape\fontsize{11pt}{14pt}\selectfont [Optional subhead — one line of context or stakes, <=20 words.]\par}
\vspace{2pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}

\vspace{8pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% RELEASE LINE + DATELINE (opens the body, AEI-style) + LEAD
% Embargo form: replace FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE with
%   EMBARGOED UNTIL [Month Day, Year, Time TZ]
% Lead = ~50 words; carries the whole story (who/what/where/why).
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\bfseries\color{accentred}\fontsize{8.25pt}{10pt}\selectfont\textls[50]{FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE}}\hspace{0.6em}{\color{muted}\fontsize{9pt}{12pt}\selectfont [CITY, State] · [Month Day, Year]}\par
\vspace{5pt}
\noindent [Lead sentence stating the finding as news. Two to three sentences that an editor could run on their own.]

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BODY  (2-4 short paragraphs; quotes interleaved)
%  - Name the paper here: ``[Exact Title],'' published in [Journal] by [Authors].
%  - Explain the method in plain language; don't name it.
%  - Keep EIL's own voice factual; put judgment in the quotes.
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent [Paragraph 1 — expand the finding. Name the paper, journal, and authors. Spell out any agency on first use.]

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent [Paragraph 2 — the method in plain language: who was compared, what records, over what period, and why the comparison is credible.]

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% QUOTE 1 (OPTIONAL) — lead author / PI: what the finding means.
% Quotes are optional. Delete this block unless you have a REAL, confirmed
% quote — never ship an invented or placeholder quote.
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent ``[Quote on what the finding means — the interpretive claim EIL's own prose avoids],'' said [Full Name], [Title], [Affiliation].

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent [Paragraph 3 — the stakes: what the finding implies for policy or practice.]

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% QUOTE 2 (OPTIONAL) — policymaker / official / stakeholder: why it matters.
% Same rule: delete unless you have a real, confirmed quote.
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent ``[Quote on why it matters from a policymaker or affected stakeholder],'' said [Full Name], [Title], [Affiliation].

\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% END MARKER
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\bfseries \#\#\#}\end{center}

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}
\vspace{4pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MEDIA CONTACT  (bottom, before boilerplate)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{muted}\fontsize{8.25pt}{11pt}\selectfont
For questions, interviews, or other media inquiries, contact [Full Name] at [email] or [phone].\par}

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BOILERPLATE  (reusable; keep identical across releases)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{muted}\fontsize{8.25pt}{11.5pt}\selectfont
{\bfseries About the Environmental Inequality Lab (EIL).} The Environmental Inequality Lab is a nonpartisan research group that applies a rigorous, data-driven approach to understand how our environment shapes economic opportunity and well-being.\par}

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% PAPER LINK  (resolvable DOI + companion research highlight)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{faint}\fontsize{7.4pt}{10pt}\selectfont
Full paper: \href{https://doi.org/[DOI]}{\color{faint}\underline{doi.org/[DOI]}} \quad·\quad
Research highlight: \href{[URL]}{\color{faint}\underline{[short link]}}\par}
```
